title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 with Major Coding and Vision Upgrades
summary: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 is now generally available, delivering significant improvements in software engineering, multimodal vision, and long-running agentic tasks.
content: Anthropic has released Claude Opus 4.7, now generally available across all Claude products, the API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry at the same price as Opus 4.6 ($5/$25 per million input/output tokens). The model delivers a notable improvement in advanced software engineering, with early testers reporting 10–15% lifts in coding task resolution and the ability to hand off complex, long-running agentic workflows with less supervision. Opus 4.7 also supports higher-resolution images up to 2,576 pixels on the long edge — over three times the prior limit — unlocking new multimodal use cases like reading dense screenshots and interpreting complex technical diagrams. As part of Anthropic's Project Glasswing cybersecurity initiative, Opus 4.7 ships with automated safeguards that detect and block prohibited cybersecurity uses, with a new Cyber Verification Program for legitimate security professionals. New features launching alongside the model include an 'xhigh' effort level for finer reasoning control, task budgets in API public beta, and a /ultrareview slash command in Claude Code.

title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.4-Cyber for Restricted Defensive Cybersecurity Use
summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.4-Cyber, a cybersecurity-focused variant of its flagship model, available on a restricted basis to vetted security organizations and researchers.
content: OpenAI unveiled GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of its latest flagship model fine-tuned specifically for defensive cybersecurity work, following rival Anthropic's announcement of Claude Mythos via Project Glasswing a week earlier. The model will be rolled out on a limited basis to vetted security vendors, organizations, and researchers due to its more permissive design on sensitive tasks such as vulnerability research and analysis. OpenAI is expanding its Trusted Access for Cyber (TAC) program — launched in February — with higher verification tiers unlocking more powerful capabilities, with GPT-5.4-Cyber reserved for the highest tier. Anthropic's Mythos had already found 'thousands' of major vulnerabilities in operating systems, web browsers, and other software through its controlled 50-partner Project Glasswing initiative. The parallel restricted releases by both companies reflect growing concern about an AI-enabled arms race in cybersecurity, where the same capabilities that help defenders find bugs can also accelerate attacks.

title: Google DeepMind Launches Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 for Physical AI
summary: Google DeepMind has released Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, a significantly upgraded reasoning model enabling robots to understand physical environments with unprecedented spatial precision.
content: Google DeepMind introduced Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, a major upgrade to its reasoning-first robotics model that enhances spatial reasoning, multi-view understanding, and task planning for real-world robots. The model adds a new instrument-reading capability — developed in close collaboration with Boston Dynamics' Spot robot — enabling robots to read complex analog gauges, pressure meters, and sight glasses for industrial facility inspection. Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 acts as the high-level 'cognitive brain' for a robot, natively calling tools like Google Search, vision-language-action models, and user-defined functions to plan and execute tasks autonomously without step-by-step human guidance. It shows significant benchmark improvements over both Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash on spatial and physical reasoning tasks including pointing, counting, and success detection. The model is DeepMind's safest robotics model to date, with superior compliance on adversarial safety tasks, and is available now to developers via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio.

title: Adobe Launches Firefly AI Assistant for Agentic Creative Workflows
summary: Adobe has launched the Firefly AI Assistant, a conversational agent that autonomously executes complex, multi-step creative tasks across Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, and other Creative Cloud apps.
content: Adobe has launched the Firefly AI Assistant, a conversational agent that orchestrates complex, multi-step workflows across Photoshop, Premiere Pro, Lightroom, Illustrator, Express, and Frame.io using natural language — eliminating the need to manually navigate specific tools or menus. Previously previewed under the codename 'Project Moonlight' at Adobe MAX in October 2025, the assistant enters public beta in the coming weeks and integrates with third-party AI models including Anthropic's Claude, Google, OpenAI, Runway, Luma AI, and ElevenLabs. Adobe calls the launch 'a fundamental shift in how creative work is done,' as the assistant learns user preferences over time — preferred tools, workflows, and aesthetic choices — to produce increasingly personalized results across sessions. Alongside the assistant, Adobe is launching Firefly Image Model 5 in public beta, Custom Models for brand-consistent private image generation, and the node-based Project Graph visual workflow system. The launch comes as CEO Shantanu Narayen prepares to step down after 18 years and Adobe faces intensifying competition from Canva (260M MAUs) and Figma.

title: White House Releases National AI Policy Framework, Seeks Federal Preemption of State Laws
summary: The Trump Administration's National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence calls on Congress to establish a unified federal AI standard that would preempt the current patchwork of conflicting state regulations.
content: On March 20, 2026, the White House released its National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence, a sweeping set of legislative recommendations intended to establish a coherent, nationally unified approach to AI governance — notably without creating any new federal regulatory body. The framework's most consequential proposal recommends that Congress preempt state AI laws that 'impose undue burdens,' seeking a single minimally burdensome national standard in place of the growing patchwork of state regulations, including Colorado's AI Act and California's CCPA automated decision-making amendments. Other key recommendations include establishing regulatory sandboxes for AI development, streamlining federal permitting for AI infrastructure, making federal datasets accessible in AI-ready formats for model training, and protecting children, creators, and vulnerable populations from AI-related harms. Speaker Mike Johnson has already called on Congress to enact legislation codifying the administration's agenda, though the framework itself carries no binding legal obligations. Businesses operating under existing state AI laws are advised to closely monitor how Congress translates these recommendations, as broad preemption language could render some currently-in-force statutes unenforceable.

title: Salesforce Expands Agent Fabric Into Full Enterprise AI Control Plane
summary: Salesforce has announced a major expansion of Agent Fabric, giving enterprises a trusted control plane to discover, govern, and orchestrate AI agents across multi-vendor environments at scale.
content: Salesforce has announced a major expansion of Agent Fabric, delivering a centralized control plane for enterprises managing hundreds of AI agents across diverse platforms including Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Foundry, and GoDaddy. The update introduces automated Agent Scanners for MCP server and platform discovery, a visual drag-and-drop authoring canvas built with MuleSoft Vibes for mapping human-in-the-loop workflows, and centralized LLM governance with AI Gateway and Trusted Agent Identity for mobile authorization on high-risk agent actions. Agent Fabric has managed thousands of agentic instances for enterprises like Capita, Alcon, and Diabsolut since its September 2025 launch, with the expansion addressing a consistent enterprise hurdle: scaling AI faster requires granular control over which processes should be fully agentic versus deterministic. The Agent Broker for deterministic orchestration enters beta this month, with full GA including the visual authoring canvas and Salesforce model support arriving in June 2026. New regional availability includes Canada and Japan, with Runtime Fabric deployment now supported via Flex Gateway.
